session.assistant: Good! I found relevant documentation. Let me read the label scheme file:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: PR and Plan Label Assignment Scheme 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding how plans, code PRs, and learn issues are labeled" 5→ - "working with erk-pr, erk-plan, erk-core, or erk-learn labels" 6→ - "debugging label filtering in TUI or CLI views" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "querying core erk PRs without using the erk-core label" 9→ warning: "Use erk-core to query non-learn PRs (plans + code). Use erk-pr for ALL erk-submitted PRs including learn. Use type-specific labels (erk-plan, erk-learn) only when you need to filter to a specific type." 10→ - action: "backfilling labels on existing issues without considering updated_at side effects" 11→ warning: "GitHub label operations change the issue's updated_at timestamp. This affects sort order in list views and may confuse users." 12→--- 13→ 14→# PR and Plan Label Assignment Scheme 15→ 16→Erk uses `erk-pr` as the **universal base label** on all erk-submitted PRs, with `erk-core` for non-learn PRs and **type-specific labels** for filtering. 17→ 18→## Label Taxonomy 19→ 20→| Label | Purpose | Applied To | 21→| ------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- | 22→| `erk-pr` | Base label — all erk-submitted PRs | All plans, learn plans, and code PRs | 23→| `erk-core` | Core work label — non-learn PRs | Plan PRs and code PRs (NOT learn PRs) | 24→| `erk-planned-pr` | Legacy base label — identifies plans | Historical plans only (no longer applied by code) | 25→| `erk-plan` | Type label — implementation plans | Regular plans | 26→| `erk-learn` | Type label — documentation plans | Learn plans | 27→| `erk-objective` | Separate system — objectives | Objective issues | 28→| `erk-consolidated` | Consolidation marker | Consolidated learn plans | 29→ 30→## Label Assignment
... [49 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I understand the labeling scheme. According to the documentation: - `erk-core` label should be applied to regular plans (line 36: "Applies `erk-core` for regular plans (NOT learn plans)") - Plans should have `["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]` for regular plans - The issue is finding plans with only `["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` (missing the `erk-core` label)
Now let me search for these specific label patterns in the test files:

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Based on my analysis of the code and the label scheme documentation, here's my report:
## Report: Plan Objects Missing `"erk-core"` Label
According to the label scheme documentation in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/label-scheme.md`, regular (non-learn) plans should have **three labels**: `["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]`.
The following test files contain Plan objects with only `["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` (missing `"erk-core"`):
### File-by-File Breakdown
**1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_filtering.py`** - Line 32 (plan1) - Line 45 (plan2) - Line 81 (open_plan) - Line 94 (closed_plan) - Line 145 (plan_with_one) - Line 193 (plan in loop, all 5 plans) - Line 257 (wrong_labels_plan) - Line 319 (plan in test_plan_list_empty_results)
**2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_pr_columns.py`** - Line 31 (plan)
**3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_impl_columns.py`** - Line 44 (plan)
**4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_action_state.py`** - Line 31 (plan1)
**5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/dash/test_workflow_run_state.py`** - Line 70 (running_plan) - Line 226 (plan)
## Summary
Total lines needing updates: **13 instances** across 5 files
All of these Plan objects should be updated from `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` to `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-core"]` to comply with the label scheme where regular plans must include the `"erk-core"` label.
